positive.label: Scandi Blonde — global lightening + tone

desired.description: Clean, icy, level 10 platinum blonde. Cool, reflective finish. No yellow.

starting.description: Medium Blonde, virgin hair, healthy condition

steps.processing_time: Up to 45 min (visual check until pale yellow)
steps.application_notes: Add 3 pumps Intensive Conditioning Serum. Apply 1cm off scalp through ends first. Roots once mids reach level 9.
Rinse lightener, shampoo, towel dry, apply toner at basin.

expected_output.colour_theory: 20 vol safely lifts virgin level 7 three levels. At level 10, underlying pigment is pale yellow. V (Violet) neutralises yellow. P (Pearl, blue-violet undertone) adds icy reflective finish.
expected_output.warnings: Monitor lightener visually — do not exceed 45 min. Apply roots last to avoid hot roots from scalp heat. Porous ends may grab toner faster; check at 10 min.

positive.why: Standard global lightening on virgin hair. 1:1 ratio for SilkLift is critical — thicker mix gives controlled lift. V+P toner targets the pale yellow exposed at level 10.

positive.label: Brassy Balayage Correction — glaze/toning only

desired.description: Creamy, cool-toned level 8/9 lived-in blonde. Natural root preserved.

starting.description: Dark Blonde roots. Grown-out balayage, mids/ends faded to uneven level 8/9 with heavy gold and slight copper (orange). Slightly porous ends.

steps.application_notes: Apply global on damp hair at basin. Focus on brassiest transition bands first. Pull through porous ends for final 5-10 min only to prevent over-toning.

expected_output.colour_theory: Colorance is acidic demi-permanent — cannot lift, so level 6 roots stay untouched (no hot roots). 8BA (Smoky Beige) contains Blue/Ash to neutralise orange/copper. 9NA (Natural Ash) neutralises yellow and provides natural balance so hair does not grab grey or go muddy.
expected_output.warnings: Porous ends will absorb faster — apply to ends last or for reduced time. Monitor brassiest sections; they may need longer. Do not exceed 20 min on porous hair.

positive.why: Toning-only correction. No lifting needed. Colorance deposits without disturbing the natural root. 8BA handles the orange, 9NA handles the yellow — together they create a cool, creamy result.

positive.label: Grey Blending with Highlift — root retouch

desired.description: Clean, bright, neutral-cool blonde at root, blending with level 10 ends. Cover grey. No bleach on scalp.

starting.description: Medium Blonde with 40% scattered grey. Mids/ends previously coloured to level 10 neutral blonde. Normal at root, slightly dry on ends.
starting.condition: normal at root, slightly dry ends

steps.application_notes: Regrowth only, cross-check sections. Do NOT overlap onto level 10 ends — breakage risk.

expected_output.colour_theory: Highlift requires 12% (40 vol) and 1:2 ratio to maximise the lifting curve and minimise deposit. At level 7, underlying pigment is yellow-orange. 11A (Ash) neutralises warmth. 11N (Natural) provides the background dye required to cover unpigmented grey hair — highlift ash alone lacks opacity for grey blending.
expected_output.warnings: Do NOT overlap onto previously lightened ends — breakage risk. 45 min is strict; cutting short will leave grey uncovered and warmth unresolved. Cross-check sections to ensure full saturation at the root.

positive.why: Client wants no bleach on scalp — highlift is the alternative. 12% and 1:2 ratio are mandatory for highlift to work. 11N provides grey coverage that 11A alone cannot. Regrowth-only application protects fragile ends.

negative.label: WRONG: 1:2 ratio for SilkLift lightener

desired.description: Clean, icy, level 10 platinum blonde. No yellow.

starting.description: Medium Blonde, virgin hair, healthy condition

negative.correction: SilkLift lightener ratio is 1:1 (35g powder : 35ml developer), not 1:2. A 1:2 ratio makes the mix too runny, reduces lifting power, and produces inconsistent results. Only Topchic highlift (11-series) uses 1:2.

negative.label: WRONG: 10V+10P instead of 8BA+9NA for brassy balayage

desired.description: Creamy, cool-toned level 8/9 lived-in blonde. Correct brassiness.

starting.description: Dark Blonde roots. Grown-out balayage, mids/ends level 8/9 with gold and copper. Slightly porous ends.

negative.correction: 10V and 10P are level 10 violet/pearl shades designed for pale yellow at level 10. This client has orange/copper at level 8/9 — violet does not neutralise orange. The correct choice is 8BA (Smoky Beige — blue/ash to neutralise orange) + 9NA (Natural Ash — neutralises yellow, adds natural balance). Match the shade level to the hair level and the tone to the unwanted pigment.

negative.label: WRONG: 9% (30 vol) and 11V instead of 12% (40 vol) and 11A for highlift

desired.description: Cover grey, stay bright blonde, no bleach on scalp.

starting.description: Medium Blonde with 40% grey. Mids/ends previously level 10. Slightly dry ends.
starting.condition: normal at root, slightly dry ends

negative.correction: Two errors: (1) Highlift REQUIRES 12% (40 vol) — 9% (30 vol) does not provide enough lift for highlift to work, leaving the result muddy and warm. (2) 11V (Violet) is the wrong tone choice. At level 7, the underlying pigment is yellow-orange. Violet neutralises yellow but does nothing for the orange component. 11A (Ash, blue-green) neutralises the warmth across the yellow-orange spectrum. Correct: Topchic 11N + 11A with 12% (40 vol).

negative.label: WRONG: vague processing time ranges
negative.correction: Processing times must be precise. 'Up to 45 min (visual check until pale yellow)' gives a clear maximum with a visual checkpoint. '15 min' is a specific toner time. Vague ranges like '20-45 min' and '15-25 min' are unhelpful — they signal uncertainty and a professional colourist cannot act on them.
